title: Clean state active execplan rows during archive
goal: Implement #522 by making archive-plan --apply-cleanup clean state.toml active rows whose execplan field points at the archived plan.
Preserve the package's existing archive command and planning cleanup model.
non_goals: Do not redesign planning state storage.
Do not add a new cleanup command.
Do not make archive-plan silently delete unrelated active work.

intent.outcome: archive-plan cleanup does not leave broken active planning state after archiving the selected plan
intent.constraints: match only active rows that point at the archived plan through known execplan/path/surface fields
intent.latitude: may extend cleanup matching and focused tests
intent.escalation: stop if cleanup would require broad TOML rewrite semantics or ambiguous deletion of unrelated active rows

execution.proof: focused archive cleanup tests, package planning tests, ruff, doctor, and planning surface checks passed

invariants: archive-plan only removes or closes rows that point at the archived plan
cleanup remains visible through actions or warnings

required_continuation.activation trigger: after #522 closes, continue the next workflow-friction-polish issue or refreshed higher-priority issue

signals found: Archive cleanup had alias drift across path/surface/plan/execplan fields.
Closed-state cleanup could bypass compact todo cleanup.
signals fixed: Added a shared active execplan reference normalizer.
Removed compact todo rows during closed-state cleanup.
signals dismissed: No new issue for plan closeout prose overhead; existing structured-first lanes already cover that broader friction.
